query: What characteristics distinguish nuclear export signals in different proteins and how do these signals interact with the export receptor CRM1 to regulate nuclear-cytoplasmic trafficking?

schema.explanation: Identifies the specific protein being studied to understand its nuclear export characteristics.
Helps determine the presence and characteristics of NES in different proteins.
Reveals how NES interacts with the export receptor CRM1 to regulate nuclear-cytoplasmic trafficking.
Specifies the sequence motif that mediates the binding of proteins to CRM1 for nuclear export.
Explains how the interaction between NES and CRM1 regulates the movement of proteins between the nucleus and cytoplasm.
Measures how efficiently proteins are exported from the nucleus to the cytoplasm.
Describes alternative mechanisms by which proteins can be localized in the cytoplasm.
Contrasts with NES by identifying signals that direct proteins to the nucleus.
Compares the sequences of different proteins to identify conserved NES motifs.
Assesses how specific inhibitors affect nuclear export to understand the role of CRM1 and NES in this process.
Analyzes the conservation of NES sequences across different proteins and species.
Examines the expression level of CRM1 in different cell types and conditions.
Studies the rate and dynamics of nuclear export for different proteins.
Investigates the interactions between proteins containing NES and other proteins involved in nuclear-cytoplasmic trafficking.
Determines the subcellular localization of proteins containing NES under different conditions.
Examines the effect of post-translational modifications on NES function and nuclear export.
Analyzes the cell type specificity of NES-mediated nuclear export and its regulation.
